title: What permissions need to be set for additionalimagestore ?
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I have two users on my system. With one of them I create an world accessible (read only) new image store via the `--root` flag. Then I add this image store to the other user as an additionalimagestore with the config as in [#6777](https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/6777#issuecomment-650116931). When I run `podman images` with the second user I get an permission denied error, that does not make a lot of sense...

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a new folder with user one, that is accessible for all other users.

2. Make the new folder a storage and pull an image into that storage: `podman --root /full/path/to/folder pull registry.fedoraproject.org/f29/httpd`.

3. Create a config file for second user as mentioned above.

4. run `podman images` with second user. This gives the error
   > Error: error opening "/full/path/to/folder/overlay-images/images.lock": permission denied

5. Fix the error: run `chmod go+rwx /full/path/to/folder/overlay-images/images.lock` and `chmod go+rwx /full/path/to/folder/overlay-layers/layers.lock`

6. Check result with 2nd user: `podman images` gives the same error.

7. Make the whole folder read/write: `sudo chmod go+rw -R /full/path/to/folder`. Sudo is needed since some of the files in the storage container belong to different users, etc. due to the creation throuhg podman. Maybe using podman unshare would be a better approach? 

8. Check result with 2nd user: `podman images` gives still the same error.

9. Make the whole folder read/write/execute: `sudo chmod go+xrw -R /full/path/to/folder`.

10. `podman images` gives the expected result:

   > REPOSITORY                            TAG     IMAGE ID      CREATED        SIZE    ReadOnly
   > registry.fedoraproject.org/f29/httpd  latest  25c76f9dcdb5  14 months ago  482 MB  true


**Describe the results you received:**
In all cases the error message is regarding to the file /full/path/to/folder/overlay-images/images.lock. This file is read/write/execute for all users from step 5 on. The error statement is unprecise, since the error comes from other files. These other files are made read/write/executable in step 9. Than it works. Making them just read/write for every user does not solve the problem for the 2nd user.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Step 6 should work or give a different error message.

It would be very good to know, which of the additional storage files need read/write/execute permissions and which just need read permission.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.0
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.13.4
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

```

comments.body:
The problem is the lock file is being rewritten each time you update the local share, I believe, so everytime you pull you need to 
chown a+rx -R rootfs

I recently got a PR into container/storage to fix this problem,https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/652
But this fix has not bubbled its way up into buildah/podman yet.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This is now fixed in master.
